---
title: 'Tongues | The Fulfillment'
date: '2015-04-22T04:10:00.0000000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: 'Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '4041723914787417065'
blogger_filename: '/2015/04/tongues-fulfillment.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'Spiritual Growth'
---

<br /><br /><br /><br /><table align="center" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" class="tr-caption-container" style="margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto; text-align: center;"><tbody><tr><td><a href="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/all-rights-reserved-lion-tongue-by-doug-wheller-505f8f8391f6.jpg" style="margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto;"><img alt="All rights reserved &quot;Lion tongue&quot; by Doug Wheller       1 Now concerning spiritual gifts, brethren, I do not want you to be ignorant" border="0" height="320" src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/all-rights-reserved-lion-tongue-by-doug-wheller-505f8f8391f6.jpg" title="All rights reserved &quot;Lion tongue&quot; by Doug Wheller      1 Now concerning spiritual gifts, brethren, I do not want you to be ignorant" width="305" /></a></td></tr><tr><td class="tr-caption" style="font-size: 12.8px;"><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11pt; margin: 0in;">All rights reserved&nbsp;<a href="https://flic.kr/p/feCWYU" target="_blank">"Lion tongue" by Doug Wheller</a></div></td></tr></tbody></table><span xmlns=""></span><br /><br /><br /><ul style="text-align: left;"><li><a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/2015/04/tongues-foundation.html" target="_blank">Tongues | The Foundation</a></li><li><a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/2015/04/tongues-foreshadowing.html" target="_blank">Tongues | The Foreshadowing</a></li><li><a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/2015/04/tongues-fulfillment.html" target="_blank">Tongues | The Fulfillment</a></li><li><a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/2015/04/tongues-facts.html" target="_blank">Tongues | The Facts</a></li><li><a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/2015/04/tongues-familiar.html" target="_blank">Tongues | The Familiar</a></li></ul><div><br /><span xmlns=""></span> <br /><div style="border-width: 100%; direction: ltr;"><div style="direction: ltr; margin-left: 0in; margin-top: 0in; width: 6.0861in;"><div style="direction: ltr; margin-left: 0in; margin-top: 0in; width: 6.0861in;"><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 18pt; margin: 0in;"><span style="background: white; font-weight: bold;">Tongues What's that about?</span></div><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11pt; margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-top: 0pt;"><br /></div><div style="margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-left: 0.375in; margin-top: 0pt;"><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/nkjv/1-corinthians/12.html"><span style="background: white; font-size: 12pt; font-style: italic;">1 Corinthians 12</span></a></div><div style="font-size: 12pt; margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-left: 0.375in; margin-top: 0pt;"><span style="font-style: italic;">1 Now concerning spiritual gifts, brethren, I do not want you to be ignorant</span></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><span style="background: white; font-weight: bold;">The Gifts of the Spirit</span></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><span style="background: white;">There are several lists in the New Testament about gifts; they do not speak about the same thing or to the same people. Neither are any of the lists meant to be exclusive or all encompassing. Rather, these are to give us some functional insight into the common ways God works in our livesâ€¦ while leaving room for God to be God outside his box. </span></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><span style="background: white;">The primary section in all the bible commonly studied for The Gift of Tongues is&nbsp;</span><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/1-corinthians/passage.aspx?q=1+corinthians+12;1+corinthians+13;1+corinthians+14"><span style="background: white;">1 Corinthians 12-14</span></a>. As we've seen from Part 1 and Part 2 of this study, it's not anywhere near the only place it's mentioned. But this section of scripture is dedicated by Paul to address this topic of Tongues and the other gifts of the Spirit. We will dive in and take it all apart and look at it on the insideâ€¦ </div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;">But first, we must take a step back, and see the big picture. </div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;">Paul is addressing the Church of Corinth. These are a wild bunch. Prone to wild things. These are the ones that Paul had to dress down in chapter 5 for having a person who had taken his father's wife. This is a wild bunch.&nbsp;They are excited about this Tongues deal and the other gifts. Paul makes a VERY important statement here.</div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="margin-left: .375in; margin: 0in;"><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/nkjv/1-corinthians/12.html"><span style="font-size: 13.5pt;">1 Corinthians 12</span></a></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in 0in 0in 0.375in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-left: 0.375in; margin-top: 0pt;"><span style="font-style: italic;">1 Now concerning spiritual gifts, brethren, I do not want you to be ignorant</span></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;">There is no excuse to be ignorant of spiritual&nbsp;gifts in this day and age. We not only have the&nbsp;bible, and 2,000 years of&nbsp;Church history. We have, in the&nbsp;last 100 plus years have had more&nbsp;people walking in the miracle powers of God&nbsp;at one time than&nbsp;in the history of the&nbsp;world. </div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;">Starting from the day Martin Luther posted his statement that men are saved by&nbsp;Faith,&nbsp;God has been restoring the basics of Christian Living to the&nbsp;Body of Christ one revelation at a time. </div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;">Slowly but surely, Religion is&nbsp;being&nbsp;cast out for the evil spirit it is and replaced with the simple truths of God's&nbsp;Word.</div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in 0in 0in 0.375in;">As Jesse&nbsp;Duplantis often says: "<span style="font-style: italic;">It takes a good theologian to help you misunderstand the bible.</span>" </div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;">So, we have no excuse to be ignorant. There are many great teachers of the Word. There are many who walk in these gifts in varying degrees today. Find&nbsp;someone who does and learn from them.</div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;">This set of chapters (1 Corinthians 12-14) can be divided into sections. </div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><ul style="direction: ltr; margin-bottom: 0in; margin-left: 0.375in; margin-top: 0in; unicode-bidi: embed;" type="disc"><li style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-top: 0px; vertical-align: middle;"><span style="font-size: 13.5pt;">Admonition to not      be willing ignorant of this subject. </span></li><li style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-top: 0px; vertical-align: middle;"><span style="font-size: 13.5pt;">Origin - Chapter 12      primarily addresses the origin and power of the gifts and putâ€™s them forth      in a list. </span></li><li style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-top: 0px; vertical-align: middle;"><span style="font-size: 13.5pt;">Power - Chapter 13      speaks about where the true power in these gifts is found; In Love. </span></li><li style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-top: 0px; vertical-align: middle;"><span style="font-size: 13.5pt;">Effectiveness/Use -      Chapter 14 goes on to break down the one on one aspects, personal prayer,      and more on group dynamics. </span></li></ul><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><span style="font-weight: bold;">All In All</span></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;">One of my favorite scriptures in the bible, 1 Corinthians 12:6, settles forever any lingering debate in my mind that any of the gifts, especially Tongues, were for some people and not for others. The same God works ALL IN ALL. </div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in 0in 0in 0.375in;"><span style="font-style: italic;">All means all, and that's all "all" means. </span></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;">That means that all the gifts are worked in all the people. However itâ€™s clear from the rest of the book that the Corinthians were using this to their ruin by having all the people speaking in Tongues all the time. The very fact that they were all doing this is evidence that it's available to all people. </div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;">No gift, by itself, meets all the needs of the people present. Therefore, in a group/ministry setting, no gift should be used exclusive unto itself without the others. This is why in chapter 14, verse 26 Paul concludes the three chapter section by saying that all of the gifts should be used, a few at a time, for the whole church to be edified. Fittingly and in order. </div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><span style="font-weight: bold;">Lists and More Lists</span></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;">There are many â€œgiftâ€ lists in the New Testament. Some writers simply dump everything that mentions the word â€œgiftâ€ into one box and call them all the same thing. But the bible puts these lists in separate places on their own because they are different things. </div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;">So let us take a look at these lists briefly. A note to make, itâ€™s quite simple to understand these lists if you actually read the whole section. Each list is found within a context (leadership, body of Christ, etc). Each list begins by a brief description of itâ€™s content and intent. </div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="margin: 0in;"><span style="font-size: 13.5pt;">Here is a another great article on this topic: </span><a href="http://iblp.org/questions/what-are-three-categories-spiritual-gifts"><span style="font-family: &quot;calibri&quot;; font-size: 11pt;">http://iblp.org/questions/what-are-three-categories-spiritual-gifts</span></a></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><span style="font-weight: bold;">Manifestations of the Holy Spirit. </span></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;">1Cor 12:7-11 gives us a specific list of nine gifts. Paul calls these the manifestations of the Holy Spirit. What does that mean? The Holy Spirit is always there, heâ€™s everywhere. But He isnâ€™t always physically noticeably there. When HolySpirit shows up in a way that can be tangible He brings certain characteristics with Him to the environment. </div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;">Just as when a grumpy person shows up they bring a certain atmosphere with them, and when a cheerful person shows up they bring a certain atmosphere with them. â€œManifestations of the Holy Spiritâ€ means that when the Holy Spirit shows up, He is likely going to bring these primary characteristics with him. He works all of them. They are His gifts to His people. He may work some or all. He works out what, who, and when. It will always be in His order. And you canâ€™t truly have them without Him involved. </div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in 0in 0in 0.375in;">1 Corinthians 12</div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in 0in 0in 0.375in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in 0in 0in 0.375in;"><span style="font-style: italic;">7 But the manifestation of the Spirit is given to each one for the profit of all: 8 for to one is given the word of </span><span style="font-style: italic; font-weight: bold;">wisdom</span><span style="font-style: italic;"> through the Spirit, to another the </span><span style="font-style: italic; font-weight: bold;">word of knowledge</span><span style="font-style: italic;"> through the same Spirit, 9 to another </span><span style="font-style: italic; font-weight: bold;">faith</span><span style="font-style: italic;"> by the same Spirit, to another </span><span style="font-style: italic; font-weight: bold;">gifts of healings</span><span style="font-style: italic;"> by the same Spirit, 10 to another the </span><span style="font-style: italic; font-weight: bold;">working of miracles</span><span style="font-style: italic;">, to another </span><span style="font-style: italic; font-weight: bold;">prophecy</span><span style="font-style: italic;">, to another </span><span style="font-style: italic; font-weight: bold;">discerning of spirits</span><span style="font-style: italic;">, to another </span><span style="font-style: italic; font-weight: bold;">different kinds of tongues</span><span style="font-style: italic;">, to another the </span><span style="font-style: italic; font-weight: bold;">interpretation of tongues</span><span style="font-style: italic;">. 11 But one and the </span><span style="font-style: italic; font-weight: bold;">same Spirit works all these things</span><span style="font-style: italic;">, distributing to each one individually as He wills.</span></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><span style="font-weight: bold;">Motivational Gifts | Appointments - God's Gifts To The Church</span></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;">There are a slightly different set of gifts mentioned later in chapter 12. This list is specifically addressing appointed gifts. These are not the random things that may happen in the midst of a service or gathering, these are gifts given to certain ones. These are the things people are gifted to BE not just DO. </div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;">These are appointments within the Church body, which Paul just got finished expounding upon. Some people are called to take the gifts of the Spirit listed earlier and the Word of God and add their supply to the Church in a more official capacity. These gifts are located here. </div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in 0in 0in 0.375in;">1 Corinthians 12</div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in 0in 0in 0.375in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in 0in 0in 0.375in;"><span style="font-style: italic;">28 And </span><span style="font-style: italic; font-weight: bold;">God has appointed these in the church</span><span style="font-style: italic;">: first </span><span style="font-style: italic; font-weight: bold;">apostles</span><span style="font-style: italic;">, second </span><span style="font-style: italic; font-weight: bold;">prophets</span><span style="font-style: italic;">, third </span><span style="font-style: italic; font-weight: bold;">teachers</span><span style="font-style: italic;">, after that </span><span style="font-style: italic; font-weight: bold;">miracles</span><span style="font-style: italic;">, then gifts of </span><span style="font-style: italic; font-weight: bold;">healings</span><span style="font-style: italic;">, </span><span style="font-style: italic; font-weight: bold;">helps</span><span style="font-style: italic;">, </span><span style="font-style: italic; font-weight: bold;">administrations</span><span style="font-style: italic;">, varieties of </span><span style="font-style: italic; font-weight: bold;">tongues</span><span style="font-style: italic;">.</span></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><span style="font-weight: bold;">Ministry Position Gifts: Leadership roles of the Church</span></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;">These gifts are also listed in a more specific list in Ephesians 4. This list however is specifically referring to the leadership gifts. These five positions: Apostle, Prophet, Evangelist, Pastor, and Teacher are given specifically to the entire body of Christ as the ones charged with preparing them for the work of ministry and helping them grow up and mature. </div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;">Notice here that it is the BODY OF CHRIST that should be ministering and the ministry gifts are given to prepare them for it. So often, especially in the USA, we consider â€œMinistersâ€ as the ones who are hired to do full time ministry. These are the ones who make their living writing books and preaching. But thatâ€™s not the bible definition, really. We are all ministers. The writing and the preaching is supposed to be preparing the Body of Christ in the USA to minister. Church services are classrooms. Real Life ministry should be taking place, <span style="font-style: italic;">mostly</span>, outside the Church. Malls, Stores, Grocery Shopping, Elevatorsâ€¦ thatâ€™s where the majority of the healing, salvations, etc ought to be taking place. </div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;">&nbsp;&nbsp;</div><div style="margin-left: .375in; margin: 0in;"><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/nkjv/ephesians/passage.aspx?q=ephesians+4:6-16"><span style="font-size: 13.5pt;">Ephesians 4:7-16</span></a></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in 0in 0in 0.375in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in 0in 0in 0.375in;"><span style="font-style: italic;">7 But to each one of us grace was given according to the measure of Christ's gift. 8 Therefore He says: "When He ascended on high, He led captivity captive, And gave gifts to men." 9 (Now this, "He ascended"--what does it mean but that He also first descended into the lower parts of the earth? 10 He who descended is also the One who ascended far above all the heavens, that He might fill all things.) 11 And </span><span style="font-style: italic; font-weight: bold;">He Himself gave some to be apostles, some prophets, some evangelists, and some pastors and teachers, </span><span style="font-style: italic;">12 </span><span style="font-style: italic; font-weight: bold;">for the equipping of the saints</span><span style="font-style: italic;"> for the work of ministry, for the edifying of the body of Christ, 13 till we all come to the unity of the faith and of the knowledge of the Son of God, to a perfect man, to the measure of the stature of the fullness of Christ; 14 that we should no longer be children, tossed to and fro and carried about with every wind of doctrine, by the trickery of men, in the cunning craftiness of deceitful plotting, 15 but, speaking the truth in love, may grow up in all things into Him who is the head--Christ-- 16 from whom the whole body, joined and knit together by what every joint supplies, according to the effective working by which every part does its share, causes growth of the body for the edifying of itself in love.</span></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in 0in 0in 0.375in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><span style="font-weight: bold;">Measure of GRACE-Charis(ma):</span></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;">Paul starts this section off by talking about the Body of Christ. Just as before. Here he tells us to be sober in our self-judgment or self-evaluation. This has been abused by preachers and people to think that you should beat yourself up and call yourself a dog. This type of teaching is contrary to the teaching of this verse. We should be sober, not emotional or lead by outside influences, in our evaluation of who we are and what we do. This means we should not think more lowly of ourselves OR more highly. We throw out our opinion of ourselves altogether and replace it with Godâ€™s opinion of us. Which by the way, is much higher than our own usually. </div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;">Then we see that we are given grace/gifting to do these things. All of these things are things we, as the body, should be doing. However when we do them, we should do them at the level and ability we have developed through God. Some are called and gifted more highly in a specific area than an another. It is the GRACE of God that is the primary subject of this section of scripture not the gifts. It is this GRACE which should be on our minds as we do everything we do. We all have these gifts, but these gifts are going to differ in their function in us according to the level of GRACE we walk in. </div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;">GRACE can be defined as Godâ€™s supernatural ability. The word GRACE in verse 3 is <a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/lexicons/greek/kjv/charis.html">Charis</a> (GRACE). The words used during the rest of the chapter translated gifts are <a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/lexicons/greek/kjv/charisma.html">Charis-ma</a> (Gracious Gifts), this is from the root of Charis. This is all talking about the same thing. This list is a list of the ways GRACE can operate through us, and how the measure of our understanding of GRACE we walk in, determines how well we walk in these gifts. </div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;">Therefore, if you teach, teach with GRACE on your mind. If you give, give liberally, because of the GRACE of God on your life. If you show mercy, do it with cheerfulness. Why? Because there are people who give mercy and forgiveness with a frown on their face. â€œIâ€™ll forgive but I wonâ€™t forget!â€, they say. However that is not forgiveness. Jesus tells us about the man who was forgiven much and chose to punish someone who owed him little. That man wasnâ€™t walking in the GRACE he received.</div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="margin-left: .375in; margin: 0in;"><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/nkjv/romans/passage.aspx?q=romans+12:3-13"><span style="font-size: 13.5pt;">Romans 12:3-13</span></a></div><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11pt; margin-left: .375in; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in 0in 0in 0.375in;"><span style="font-style: italic;">3 </span><span style="font-style: italic; font-weight: bold;">For I say, through the </span><span style="font-style: italic; font-weight: bold; text-decoration: underline;">grace</span><span style="font-style: italic; font-weight: bold;"> given to me, to everyone who is among you, not to think of himself more highly than he ought to think, but to think soberly, as God has dealt to each one a measure of faith</span><span style="font-style: italic;">. 4 </span><span style="font-style: italic; font-weight: bold;">For as we have many members in one body, but all the members do not have the same function</span><span style="font-style: italic;">, 5 so we, being many, are one body in Christ, and individually members of one another. 6 </span><span style="font-style: italic; font-weight: bold;">Having then gifts differing according to the grace that is given to us</span><span style="font-style: italic;">, let us use them: if </span><span style="font-style: italic; font-weight: bold;">prophecy</span><span style="font-style: italic;">, let us prophesy in proportion to our faith; 7 or </span><span style="font-style: italic; font-weight: bold;">ministry</span><span style="font-style: italic;">, let us use it in our ministering; he who </span><span style="font-style: italic; font-weight: bold;">teaches</span><span style="font-style: italic;">, in teaching; 8 he who </span><span style="font-style: italic; font-weight: bold;">exhorts</span><span style="font-style: italic;">, in exhortation; he who </span><span style="font-style: italic; font-weight: bold;">gives</span><span style="font-style: italic;">, with liberality; he who </span><span style="font-style: italic; font-weight: bold;">leads</span><span style="font-style: italic;">, with diligence; he who shows </span><span style="font-style: italic; font-weight: bold;">mercy</span><span style="font-style: italic;">, with cheerfulness. 9 Let </span><span style="font-style: italic; font-weight: bold;">love</span><span style="font-style: italic;"> be without hypocrisy. Abhor what is evil. Cling to what is good. 10 Be kindly affectionate to one another with brotherly love, in honor giving preference to one another; 11 not lagging in diligence, fervent in spirit, serving the Lord; 12 </span><span style="font-style: italic; font-weight: bold;">rejoicing in hope, patient in tribulation, continuing steadfastly in prayer;</span><span style="font-style: italic;"> 13 </span><span style="font-style: italic; font-weight: bold;">distributing to the needs of the saints, given to hospitality.</span></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><span style="font-weight: bold;">Cultivate The Gifts</span></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;">Paul encourages us several times throughout 1 Corinthians chapters 12-14 to desire and seek the gifts of God. Each of us should be ready at a moments notice to be used of the Holy Spirit in these gifts for the building up of the Body of Christ. However, if you havenâ€™t spent your time praying and cultivating yourself in the Love of God and GRACE of Jesus you are not as likely going to be ready to be used in these ways. </div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;">These scriptures, 1 Corinthians 12-14, Romans 12, and Ephesians 4, are the primary places that list out gifts. The word â€œgiftâ€ or â€œgiftsâ€ can be found many other places, and a thorough study of all of those could be interesting. The main point here is to remember what these lists pertain to in context for a proper understanding of who they pertain to and how to cultivate them. </div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><div style="font-size: medium;">*</div><blockquote class="tr_bq" style="font-size: medium;"><b>Tweet These:</b><br />#<a href="http://ctt.ec/8E5X7" target="_blank">TellYourStory</a>#<a href="http://ctt.ec/5xc67" target="_blank">BeStillBeLed</a></blockquote><div style="font-size: medium;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: medium;">More about&nbsp;<a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/2014/10/be-still-and-be-led-by-his-still-small.html" target="_blank">BeStillBeLed</a>&nbsp;&lt;<a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/2014/10/be-still-and-be-led-by-his-still-small.html" target="_blank">here</a>&gt;</div><h4 style="font-size: medium;">And now I leave you with a question:</h4><blockquote class="tr_bq" style="font-size: medium;"><b>So... what are your thoughts about this? What did this make you think about?&nbsp;<a class="g-profile" href="https://plus.google.com/106337514196493406365" target="_blank"></a></b></blockquote><div style="font-size: medium;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: medium;"><b>Hit me up on Twitter&nbsp;<a href="https://twitter.com/darrellwolfe" target="_blank">@DarrellWolfe&nbsp;</a>or on Google&nbsp;<a class="g-profile" href="https://plus.google.com/106337514196493406365" target="_blank">+DarrellWolfe</a></b></div><h4 style="font-size: medium;"><g:plus action="share"></g:plus></h4><h4 style="font-size: medium;">By&nbsp;<a href="https://plus.google.com/+DarrellWolfe/posts" target="_blank">Darrell Wolfe</a></h4><div style="font-size: medium;"><ul><li><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/p/about-20.html" target="_blank">New Here? Start HERE</a></span></li><li><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/p/got-freedom.html" target="_blank">Got LIFE?</a></span></li><li><a href="http://eepurl.com/MzAVr" target="_blank">Insight In Your Inbox: Sign up for email delivery here</a>.&nbsp;</li><li>Get My Book: "<a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/p/banking-basics-for-believers-book-by.html" target="_blank">Banking Basics for Believers</a>", which guides you through the basics of banking and money from a Christian Perspective.&nbsp;</li></ul></div><div class="separator" style="clear: both; font-size: medium; text-align: center;"><a href="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/cover-art-e0f7cc956c34.png" style="margin-left: 1em; margin-right: 1em;"><img border="0" height="232" src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/cover-art-e0f7cc956c34.png" width="320" /></a></div></div></div></div></div></div>
